actual_current_surface_status: no-go / exact negative boundary for the current PR230 surface: microscopic source/transfer/Feshbach formalism reduces K'(pole) or pole residue to a missing same-surface derivative/projection row

assumptions_exercise.assumption: same-surface analytic inverse kernel K(x) exists near the scalar pole
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: the residue formula is only formal; source functional derivatives do not define K'(pole)
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: turn the source two-point function into a meromorphic inverse-kernel problem

assumptions_exercise.assumption: the pole is isolated and simple after the required FVIR/thermodynamic order
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: continuous threshold or multipole behavior replaces a scalar LSZ residue by nonlocal spectral data
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: replace the resolvent by a one-pole Laurent term with a rank-one spectral projection

assumptions_exercise.assumption: left/right pole vectors or the spectral projection are defined on the same surface
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: a denominator zero alone has no normalization and no physical residue
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: evaluate the numerator <u,r><l,u> and the derivative sandwich <l,K'(x*)r>

assumptions_exercise.assumption: source carrier normalization from the additive Cl(3)/Z3 scalar source is the source coordinate used in K
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: the source residue can be rescaled without changing the pole location
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: prevent moving factors between source coordinate, operator, and numerator

assumptions_exercise.assumption: Feshbach/Schur P/Q split is a certified neutral scalar kernel split, not a source-only reconstruction
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: Schur algebra is correct but its rows are labels without authority
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: interpret A/B/C rows and their derivatives as same-surface operator rows

assumptions_exercise.assumption: contact subtraction is fixed before taking the pole derivative
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: finite contact choices can change inverse-curvature reads without changing finite source support
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: separate analytic contact curvature from the nonanalytic pole residue

assumptions_exercise.assumption: analytic continuation from Euclidean transfer data to the pole variable is authorized
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: transfer energies or Euclidean moments do not define the required p^2 derivative
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: identify the derivative variable x used in K'(x*) with the scalar LSZ pole variable

assumptions_exercise.assumption: FVIR, toron, and thermodynamic limits commute with pole isolation and residue extraction
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: atomless soft-continuum limits can erase the finite-volume pole residue
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: promote finite-volume spectral sums to a strict scalar pole row

assumptions_exercise.assumption: canonical O_H/source-overlap or strict physical response authority is supplied separately
assumptions_exercise.if_wrong: the route at most normalizes O_sp, not the canonical Higgs radial field
assumptions_exercise.needed_for: turn a source-pole residue into the top-Yukawa Higgs normalization used by PR230

conditional_surface_status: conditional-support if future work supplies an analytic same-surface inverse kernel or transfer resolvent with an isolated simple pole, the spectral projection/source-overlap row, the K'(pole) derivative sandwich or Feshbach block derivative rows, contact/FVIR authority, and canonical O_H or strict physical-response authority
exact_next_action: Do not replay source-functional, finite spectral, fixed-carrier, or finite-moment shortcuts.  A positive route must produce the same-surface derivative/projection row itself, or strict source-Higgs/physical-response pole rows with FVIR/contact and canonical O_H authority.
exact_operator_row_whose_absence_blocks_: transfer row: <O_s Omega, P_* O_s Omega> plus isolated-pole/FVIR authority
Feshbach rows: P K' P, P K' Q, Q K' P, Q K' Q in the certified source/orthogonal split
canonical bridge row: O_sp to O_H overlap or strict physical W/Z response

feshbach_derivative_formula.denominator: D_eff = P K P - P K Q R Q K P
feshbach_derivative_formula.derivative: D_eff' = P K' P - P K' Q R Q K P - P K Q R Q K' P + P K Q R Q K' Q R Q K P
missing_rows: P K'(x*) Q and Q K'(x*) P
Q K'(x*) Q inside the reduced resolvent sandwich
same-surface P/Q neutral scalar kernel split

first_principles_minimal_driver.blocked_shortcut: Blocks57-64 provide support around the source functional, finite spectral positivity, source carrier, and finite moments.  They do not supply the derivative/projection row.

denominator_zero.not_enough_because: zeros do not determine Laurent residues

derivative_at_zero.not_enough_because: it must be paired with a same-surface source numerator/projection
derivative_at_zero.role: fixes the inverse-propagator slope and therefore the denominator part of the residue

numerator_or_source_normalization.not_enough_because: source-pole normalization still does not identify canonical O_H
numerator_or_source_normalization.role: fixes the source overlap with the pole state
numerator_or_source_normalization.row: N* = <u,r><l,u>, or N*=1 only after a certified source-coordinate Schur normalization

same_surface_primitives: pole projection or left/right pole vectors
K'(x*) derivative row in the physical pole variable
source vector u from the compact additive scalar source
contact subtraction and FVIR/toron limiting order
canonical O_H/source-overlap or strict physical response authority

literature_and_mathematics_search.role: supports the distinction between formal projection identities and explicit projection rows

literature_and_mathematics_search.reference: analytic Fredholm theorem, standard operator-theory theorem

operator_residue_identity.general_formula: Res_x=<u,r><l,u>/<l,K'(x*)r> for a simple pole of K(x)^(-1)
operator_residue_identity.source_pole_operator_limit: unit residue can be obtained by O_sp normalization, but this is not canonical O_H authority

proposal_allowed_reason: The formal residue theorem is conditional and exact, but the current PR230 surface does not contain the load-bearing same-surface pole derivative/projection row.  The executable operator witness keeps K(pole), source vector, nullvector, and denominator zero fixed while changing K'(pole) and the residue.

same_pole_operator_witness.description: All rows have the same K(0), source vector, Feshbach zero, and nullvector at the pole.  Only the same-surface derivative row K'(0) changes, so the residue changes.

strict_non_claims: does not claim retained or proposed_retained top-Yukawa closure
does not use finite-prefix moments, Pade rows, or finite endpoint secants as residue authority
does not infer Schur A/B/C rows from source-only C_ss data
does not identify O_s or O_sp with canonical O_H
does not use H_unit, Ward, y_t_bare, observed targets, alpha_LM, plaquette/u0, kappa_s=1, c2=1, or Z_match=1
